results.core_alignment_check: actor/action/object/recipient align: both require the processor to inform the controller of intended changes concerning addition or replacement of sub-processors

results.contract_source_text: In the case of general written authorisation, the Processor shall inform the Controller of any intended changes concerning the addition or replacement of Sub-processors, thereby giving the Controller the opportunity to object to such changes. However, the Controller cannot object to any intended changes concerning the addition or replacement of Sub-processors, if the new Sub-processor provides sufficient guarantees with respect to implementation of appropriate technical and organizational measures in such a manner that the Processing will meet the requirements of the GDPR as outlined in this Agreement. If the Controller does not object to changes concerning the addition or replacement of Sub-processors within 30 days from the Processor’s notification of the intended changes, such changes shall be deemed to be accepted by the Controller.

results.contract_source_text: In the case of general written authorisation, the Processor shall inform the Controller of any intended changes concerning the addition or replacement of Sub-processors, thereby giving the Controller the opportunity to object to such changes. However, the Controller cannot object to any intended changes concerning the addition or replacement of Sub-processors, if the new Sub-processor provides sufficient guarantees with respect to implementation of appropriate technical and organizational measures in such a manner that the Processing will meet the requirements of the GDPR as outlined in this Agreement. If the Controller does not object to changes concerning the addition or replacement of Sub-processors within 30 days from the Processor’s notification of the intended changes, such changes shall be deemed to be accepted by the Controller.

results.core_alignment_check: Yes. The regulation requires a measure to restore the availability and access to personal data in a timely manner after a physical or technical incident; the contract sub-item states the ability to restore the availability and access to Personal Data in a timely manner in the same incident condition. This is the same security/recovery obligation, framed as a capability within the broader technical and organisational measures duty.
results.condition_check: Compatible. Both are triggered in the event of a physical or technical incident.
results.modality_check: Compatible. The contract sub-item is a plain mandatory statement within a mandatory parent clause, which is at least as strong as the regulation's 'can' requirement for the measure.
results.other_constraints_check: Compatible. 'In a timely manner' matches the regulation's timing requirement; no conflicting manner, target, location, or cause constraints appear.
results.contract_source_text: the ability to restore the availability and access to Personal Data in a timely manner in the event of a physical or technical incident;

results.core_alignment_check: Yes. The regulation requires regularly testing/assessing/evaluating the effectiveness of technical and organisational measures for security of processing; the contract sub-item and parent context require implementing appropriate technical and organisational measures and expressly include a process for testing, assessing and evaluating their effectiveness for ensuring security of the Processing. Same obligation/substance, with the contract at least as broad.
results.condition_check: Compatible. The regulation has no special condition beyond regularity; the contract is framed as part of the security-measures obligation and does not narrow the triggering situation.
results.modality_check: Compatible. The regulation uses 'can' in the source text but the structured requirement is an inclusion duty; the contract parent clause is mandatory ('shall implement') and the sub-item is part of that mandatory obligation.
results.other_constraints_check: Compatible. 'Regularly' and 'for ensuring the security of the processing' are matched by the contract's security-of-processing wording and the included testing/assessment/evaluation process.
results.contract_source_text: a process for testing, assessing and evaluating the effectiveness of technical and organisational measures for ensuring the security of the Processing. 2. In assessing the appropriate level of security account shall be taken in particular of the risks that are presented by Processing, in particular from accidental or unlawful destruction, loss, alteration, unauthorised disclosure of, or access to Personal Data transmitted, stored or otherwise processed. 3. The Processor hosts the data, including Personal Data, of the Controller via Microsoft Cloud Azure. The online hosting Services are delivered from data centres solely situated within EU, in Ireland and the Netherlands. 4. Microsoft Azure platform provides the security measures as described on www.Processor.com. 5. Particular security measures to be taken out by the Processor under this Agreement are specified in detail on www.Processor.com (Processor's Security and Privacy Protection Policies). Appendix 2 Security of Processing This Appendix forms part of the Agreement. 1. Taking into account the state of the art, the costs of implementation and the nature, scope, context and purposes of Processing as well as the risk of varying likelihood and severity for the rights and freedoms of natural persons, the Controller and the Processor shall implement appropriate technical and organisational measures to ensure a level of security appropriate to the risk, including inter alia as appropriate: (a) encryption of Personal Data when transmitted via public networks and in connection with remote access to Controller’s systems; (b) the ability to ensure the ongoing confidentiality, integrity, availability and resilience of Processing systems and Services;

results.contract_source_text: In the case of general written authorisation, the Processor shall inform the Controller of any intended changes concerning the addition or replacement of Sub-processors, thereby giving the Controller the opportunity to object to such changes. However, the Controller cannot object to any intended changes concerning the addition or replacement of Sub-processors, if the new Sub-processor provides sufficient guarantees with respect to implementation of appropriate technical and organizational measures in such a manner that the Processing will meet the requirements of the GDPR as outlined in this Agreement. If the Controller does not object to changes concerning the addition or replacement of Sub-processors within 30 days from the Processor’s notification of the intended changes, such changes shall be deemed to be accepted by the Controller.

Appendix 2 Security of Processing This Appendix forms part of the Agreement. 1. Taking into account the state of the art, the costs of implementation and the nature, scope, context and purposes of Processing as well as the risk of varying likelihood and severity for the rights and freedoms of natural persons, the Controller and the Processor shall implement appropriate technical and organisational measures to ensure a level of security appropriate to the risk, including inter alia as appropriate: (a) encryption of Personal Data when transmitted via public networks and in connection with remote access to Controller’s systems; (b) the ability to ensure the ongoing confidentiality, integrity, availability and resilience of Processing systems and Services;

That is an audit/documentation mechanism, not the substantive obligation to impose Article 28(3) duties on sub-processors.

results.contract_source_text: If the Controller requests the Processor to document its Sub-Processor’s compliance with the obligations laid down in Article 28 of the GDPR the Processor shall be entitled to fulfil this obligation by referring Controller to the relevant audit reports or similar documentation made available by the respective Sub-Processors with information indicating that the Sub-Processor in question complies with the GDPR, provided that such audit report(s) or similar documentation shall be based on applicable, acknowledged audit standards, e.g. ISAE 3000 or 3402, ISO 27001 or similar standards. Any additional audit reporting or additional other similar documentation requested by the Controller will be at the Controller’s cost and expenses and is charged under the conditions set out in Appendix 3. 11. Appendix 2 Security of Processing This Appendix forms part of the Agreement. 1. Taking into account the state of the art, the costs of implementation and the nature, scope, context and purposes of Processing as well as the risk of varying likelihood and severity for the rights and freedoms of natural persons, the Controller and the Processor shall implement appropriate technical and organisational measures to ensure a level of security appropriate to the risk, including inter alia as appropriate: (a) encryption of Personal Data when transmitted via public networks and in connection with remote access to Controller’s systems; (b) the ability to ensure the ongoing confidentiality, integrity, availability and resilience of Processing systems and Services;

results.contract_source_text: In the case of general written authorisation, the Processor shall inform the Controller of any intended changes concerning the addition or replacement of Sub-processors, thereby giving the Controller the opportunity to object to such changes. results.condition_check: Compatible in substance: both are conditional termination rights tied to GDPR/applicable data protection law-related circumstances, though the contract's trigger is narrower/more specific (failure to agree on reasonable terms for necessary instruction changes).
results.modality_check: Equal force: 'shall be entitled to' is mandatory/right-granting and satisfies the regulatory 'shall have the right to'.
results.other_constraints_check: Timing/manner are stricter or compatible: 60 days written notice is an added procedural requirement and does not weaken the right.
results.contract_source_text: The Processor shall use reasonable endeavours to comply with any legislative changes. However, the Processor shall not be obligated to implement any change of the instructions if the Parties cannot in good faith agree to reasonable terms for the implementation. If the Parties fail to agree in good faith to reasonable terms regarding change of the instructions each Party shall be entitled to terminate this Agreement with a written notice of 60 days, provided that such changes are deemed necessary to comply with the GDPR or other applicable EU or national data protection laws and regulation. The Main Agreement and any other agreement between the Parties involving Processing of Personal Data shall automatically terminate at the same time.

results.core_alignment_check: No: the regulation requires the controller's right to object to intended changes concerning addition/replacement of sub-processors, while the contract clause gives the controller prior general written authorization to the processor to use sub-processors; that is a different obligation (authorization, not objection).
results.condition_check: The regulation applies in case of general written authorization and after the controller has been informed of intended changes; the contract clause concerns prior general written authorization but does not provide the objection right or the post-notification trigger.
results.modality_check: The contract is a plain affirmative authorization statement, not an obligation/right to object; it does not meet the required modality for the controller's objection right.
results.other_constraints_check: The timing/trigger and object differ materially: the regulation is about objecting after being informed of intended changes, whereas the contract only grants prior authorization to use sub-processors.
results.contract_source_text: days after deletion. results.core_alignment_check: The regulation requires including measures that ensure confidentiality, integrity, availability and resilience of processing systems and services. The contract requires the Processor to implement appropriate technical and organisational measures to meet GDPR requirements and protect data subjects' rights, which is a broader security/compliance obligation but does not specifically include or state the confidentiality/integrity/availability/resilience measure itself.
results.condition_check: No condition in either side that affects alignment.
results.modality_check: The contract is mandatory/committal ('agrees to'), which is not weaker than the regulation's 'can include' framing.
results.other_constraints_check: The contract's manner clause is broader and compatible, but it does not supply the specific required content about confidentiality, integrity, availability and resilience of processing systems and services.
results.contract_source_text: The Processor agrees to implement appropriate technical and organisational measures in such a manner that the Processing of the Personal Data will meet the requirements of the GDPR and other applicable national and EU data protection law and ensure the protection of the rights of the Data Subjects.

results.core_alignment_check: actor/action/object/recipient do not describe the same obligation: the regulation requires the processor to assist the controller in communicating the breach to the data subject, while the contract requires the processor to take or propose reasonable measures to mitigate consequences and the parent clause only requires the notification to describe the nature of the breach
results.condition_check: No condition in either side; compatible
results.modality_check: Contract is a plain mandatory sub-item within a parent 'shall' clause, so force is sufficient, but it still concerns a different duty
results.other_constraints_check: No timing/location/cause issues; manner 'reasonable' is not the same as assistance in communicating to the data subject
results.contract_source_text: reasonable measures taken or proposed by the Processor to mitigate the consequences of the Personal Data Breach.

results.condition_check: Compatible: both are conditioned on taking into account state of the art, costs, nature/scope/context/purposes, and risk to rights and freedoms
results.modality_check: Compatible: contract 'shall' is at least as strong as regulation 'can' in this context, and the clause is mandatory
results.other_constraints_check: Compatible: the contract adds the appropriate-security-to-risk framing and 'including inter alia as appropriate,' which is not weaker than the regulation's security objective

Appendix 2 Security of Processing This Appendix forms part of the Agreement. 1. Taking into account the state of the art, the costs of implementation and the nature, scope, context and purposes of Processing as well as the risk of varying likelihood and severity for the rights and freedoms of natural persons, the Controller and the Processor shall implement appropriate technical and organisational measures to ensure a level of security appropriate to the risk, including inter alia as appropriate: (a) encryption of Personal Data when transmitted via public networks and in connection with remote access to Controller’s systems; (b) the ability to ensure the ongoing confidentiality, integrity, availability and resilience of Processing systems and Services;

results.modality_check: shall matches shall, but modality cannot overcome the mismatch in obligation
results.other_constraints_check: timing/manner differ and the contract concerns notification to the processor, not communication to the data subject

results.core_alignment_check: actor/action/object mismatch: regulation requires the processor to process personal data without documented instructions when required by Union or Member State law; contract only permits the processor to apply all relevant means (software, servers, IT systems) unless otherwise set out in the Agreement, which is a different and narrower/different obligation
results.condition_check: not aligned: regulation is triggered by a legal requirement under Union or Member State law; contract is triggered by an internal agreement carve-out ('unless otherwise specifically is set out in the Agreement')

results.contract_source_text: The Controller hereby authorises the Processor to Process the Personal Data on behalf of the Controller on the terms and conditions set out in this Agreement. The Processor shall Process the Personal Data only on documented instructions from the Controller. The Parties agree that this Agreement shall constitute the instructions as of the date of the Agreement. The Processor may – unless otherwise specifically is set out in the Agreement – apply all relevant means, including software, servers and IT systems.

results.contract_source_text: Data relating to criminal convictions and offences (cf. GDPR art. 10). *Sensitive data includes: data revealing racial or ethnic origin, political opinions, religious and/or philosophical beliefs, trade union membership, processing of genetic data or biometric data for the purpose of uniquely identifying a natural person, data concerning health and/or data concerning a natural person's sex life or sexual orientation Please note that for the processing of sensitive data, explicit consent of the Data Subject is required. Appendix 2 Security of Processing This Appendix forms part of the Agreement. 1. Taking into account the state of the art, the costs of implementation and the nature, scope, context and purposes of Processing as well as the risk of varying likelihood and severity for the rights and freedoms of natural persons, the Controller and the Processor shall implement appropriate technical and organisational measures to ensure a level of security appropriate to the risk, including inter alia as appropriate: (a) encryption of Personal Data when transmitted via public networks and in connection with remote access to Controller’s systems; (b) the ability to ensure the ongoing confidentiality, integrity, availability and resilience of Processing systems and Services;

results.contract_source_text: In the case of general written authorisation, the Processor shall inform the Controller of any intended changes concerning the addition or replacement of Sub-processors, thereby giving the Controller the opportunity to object to such changes. However, the Controller cannot object to any intended changes concerning the addition or replacement of Sub-processors, if the new Sub-processor provides sufficient guarantees with respect to implementation of appropriate technical and organizational measures in such a manner that the Processing will meet the requirements of the GDPR as outlined in this Agreement. If the Controller does not object to changes concerning the addition or replacement of Sub-processors within 30 days from the Processor’s notification of the intended changes, such changes shall be deemed to be accepted by the Controller.

The Main Agreement and any other agreement between the Parties involving Processing of Personal Data shall automatically terminate at the same time.

results.contract_source_text: The Processor shall use reasonable endeavours to comply with any legislative changes. However, the Processor shall not be obligated to implement any change of the instructions if the Parties cannot in good faith agree to reasonable terms for the implementation. If the Parties fail to agree in good faith to reasonable terms regarding change of the instructions each Party shall be entitled to terminate this Agreement with a written notice of 60 days, provided that such changes are deemed necessary to comply with the GDPR or other applicable EU or national data protection laws and regulation. The Main Agreement and any other agreement between the Parties involving Processing of Personal Data shall automatically terminate at the same time.

Appendix 2 Security of Processing This Appendix forms part of the Agreement. 1. Taking into account the state of the art, the costs of implementation and the nature, scope, context and purposes of Processing as well as the risk of varying likelihood and severity for the rights and freedoms of natural persons, the Controller and the Processor shall implement appropriate technical and organisational measures to ensure a level of security appropriate to the risk, including inter alia as appropriate: (a) encryption of Personal Data when transmitted via public networks and in connection with remote access to Controller’s systems; (b) the ability to ensure the ongoing confidentiality, integrity, availability and resilience of Processing systems and Services;

results.contract_source_text: The Processor shall use reasonable endeavours to comply with any legislative changes. If the Parties fail to agree in good faith to reasonable terms regarding change of the instructions each Party shall be entitled to terminate this Agreement with a written notice of 60 days, provided that such changes are deemed necessary to comply with the GDPR or other applicable EU or national data protection laws and regulation. The Main Agreement and any other agreement between the Parties involving Processing of Personal Data shall automatically terminate at the same time.

results.core_alignment_check: actor/action/object/recipient: the regulation requires the DPIA to include an assessment of necessity and proportionality; the contract requires the processor to assist the controller with compliance with the DPIA obligation and prior consultation. This is an assistance duty, not the same substantive content requirement, and it does not specifically require the assessment to be included.
results.condition_check: Compatible: both are framed around the DPIA context; the contract adds 'where necessary and taking into account the nature of the Processing,' which is a reasonable qualifier and not weaker.
results.modality_check: Compatible: 'to assist' is a mandatory processor obligation and is sufficient for an assistance duty.
results.other_constraints_check: Compatible: no conflicting timing/manner/target/location/cause constraints; the contract is narrower in scope but does not contradict the regulation.
results.contract_source_text: where necessary and taking into account the nature of the Processing, to assist the Controller in its compliance with an obligation to carry out a data protection impact assessment (“DPIA”) and prior consulting of supervisory authorities where required, cf. articles 35 and 36 of the GDPR;

results.core_alignment_check: actor/action/object do not describe the same obligation: the regulation requires the processor to assist the controller with DPIA compliance, while the contract only defines 'Processor’s Services' as support and/or education services provided from time to time, which is a generic service definition and not the specific DPIA-assistance duty
results.condition_check: No condition in either side; compatible

results.other_constraints_check: Timing 'from time to time' is irrelevant to the DPIA-assistance obligation and does not supply the missing substantive content

results.contract_source_text: ‘Personal Data’ shall mean any information Processed by Processor in connection with the provision of the Processors’ Services under this Agreement relating to an identified or identifiable natural person ('Data Subject'); an identifiable person is one who can be identified, directly or indirectly, in particular by reference to an identifier such as a name, an identification number, location data, an online identifier or to one or more factors specific to his physical, physiological, genetic, mental, economic, cultural or social identity of that person;

results.contract_source_text: In the case of general written authorisation, the Processor shall inform the Controller of any intended changes concerning the addition or replacement of Sub-processors, thereby giving the Controller the opportunity to object to such changes. However, the Controller cannot object to any intended changes concerning the addition or replacement of Sub-processors, if the new Sub-processor provides sufficient guarantees with respect to implementation of appropriate technical and organizational measures in such a manner that the Processing will meet the requirements of the GDPR as outlined in this Agreement. If the Controller does not object to changes concerning the addition or replacement of Sub-processors within 30 days from the Processor’s notification of the intended changes, such changes shall be deemed to be accepted by the Controller.

results.core_alignment_check: Yes. The regulation requires the processor to take all measures required under Article 32 / ensure security of processing. The contract parent clause requires the Controller and Processor to implement appropriate technical and organisational measures to ensure a level of security appropriate to the risk, and the sub-item specifically includes the ability to restore availability and access to Personal Data in a timely manner after a physical or technical incident. This is a concrete Article 32 security measure and is substantively aligned, with the parent clause covering the broader security obligation.
results.condition_check: Compatible. Both are triggered by security/incident-related processing risk; the contract's 'in the event of a physical or technical incident' fits within Article 32 security measures.
results.modality_check: Compatible. The regulation is mandatory ('shall'); the parent clause is also mandatory ('shall implement'), and the sub-item is part of that mandatory set.
results.other_constraints_check: Compatible. The contract's 'in a timely manner' is at least as strict as the security/availability requirement, and the incident condition is consistent with the regulation's security objective.
results.contract_source_text: the ability to restore the availability and access to Personal Data in a timely manner in the event of a physical or technical incident;

Appendix 2 Security of Processing This Appendix forms part of the Agreement. 1. Taking into account the state of the art, the costs of implementation and the nature, scope, context and purposes of Processing as well as the risk of varying likelihood and severity for the rights and freedoms of natural persons, the Controller and the Processor shall implement appropriate technical and organisational measures to ensure a level of security appropriate to the risk, including inter alia as appropriate: (a) encryption of Personal Data when transmitted via public networks and in connection with remote access to Controller’s systems; (b) the ability to ensure the ongoing confidentiality, integrity, availability and resilience of Processing systems and Services;

results.modality_check: regulation is a permissive inclusion ('can include') of measures; contract is a definition with mandatory wording, but this is irrelevant because the obligations are different
results.other_constraints_check: no relevant overlap in manner/timing/target/location/cause
results.contract_source_text: ‘Personal Data Breach’ shall mean a breach of security leading to the accidental or unlawful destruction, loss, alteration, unauthorised disclosure of, or access to, Personal Data transmitted, stored or otherwise Processed;

results.core_alignment_check: actor/object mismatch: the regulation requires the DPA to contain the duration of processing, while the contract sub-item names categories of data subjects (Controller’s employees, board members and officers), which is a different content axis and does not state processing duration
results.condition_check: no condition on either side
results.modality_check: contract is only a plain sub-item naming data subjects, not an obligation to contain duration; even with parent context it does not impose the required content
results.other_constraints_check: no relevant timing/manner/location/target/cause overlap
results.contract_source_text: Controller’s employees, board members and officers

results.core_alignment_check: actor/action/object do not match: the regulation requires the DPA to contain at least one processor identity and contact details, while the contract imposes on the Controller a duty to ensure a lawful basis for processing/transfer with third-party providers; different obligation and different content.
results.condition_check: No relevant condition in either side.
results.modality_check: The contract is a plain mandatory statement, but modality is irrelevant because the obligation is different.
results.other_constraints_check: No relevant timing/manner/target/location/cause overlap.
results.contract_source_text: It is the sole responsibility and liability of the Controller to ensure the necessary basis of lawful Processing for the transfer of the Controller’s Personal Data to and from any third party provider of an integration service and/or application that is used by the Controller via Processor.com and the Processing by any third party provider of the Controller’s Personal Data in this respect. 2. Data Subjects

results.contract_source_text: The process and timeframes for deletion of Controller’s data including the Personal Data is described in Appendix 4, which forms an integral part of the Agreement.

results.core_alignment_check: actor/action/object do not match: the regulation requires the DPA to contain the duration of processing, while the contract sub-item only lists a type of service (online data processing services/survey and reporting tool) and does not state any processing duration
results.condition_check: No condition in the regulation; contract condition is only a parent scope qualifier and does not supply the missing duration
results.modality_check: Contract sub-item is a plain descriptive statement under a mandatory parent, but modality is irrelevant because the substantive object is different
